entries.title: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 1: Plaintext Interception
entries.summary: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 1: Plaintext Interception > Project Context: Building a 3-act MITM demonstration for the CyberPeace Foundation assignment. > Act 1 establishes the baseline — showing how unencrypted TCP traffic is trivially intercepted.
concepts: Set up the full project scaffolding from scratch
Built and tested `crypto_utils.py` — all three cryptographic primitives (RSA, AES-GCM, SHA-256 fingerprint)
Wrote `server.py` — functional TCP relay accepting two clients
Wrote `alice.py` and `bob.py` in plain mode — end-to-end chat working over the relay
Configured Burp Suite upstream proxy and verified traffic routing
Captured Wireshark evidence screenshot — plaintext clearly visible in packet capture
topics: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 1: Plaintext Interception
Challenge Solved — Burp Upstream Proxy Not Forwarding

entries.title: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 2 Base: RSA Key Exchange + AES-GCM Encryption
entries.summary: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 2 Base: RSA Key Exchange + AES-GCM Encryption > Project Context: Day 2 of the MITM Lab. Today's goal was to add real encryption to Alice and Bob > so that Act 2 can demonstrate that encryption *alone* (without key verification) is not enough.
concepts: Added `encrypted` mode flag to `alice.py` — RSA keypair gen, key exchange, AES session setup
Added `encrypted` mode flag to `bob.py` — receives and processes key exchange, decrypts session key
Tested direct encrypted chat (bypassing MITM) — confirmed Wireshark shows ciphertext
Verified full key exchange handshake works end-to-end over the relay server
Debugged and resolved the race condition using a typed blocking receive loop
topics: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 2 Base: RSA Key Exchange + AES-GCM Encryption
Challenge Solved — Race Condition in Key Exchange

entries.title: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 2 Complete: MITM Key Swap
entries.summary: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 2 Complete: MITM Key Swap > Project Context: Day 3. Today's goal was to build the actual attacker — a MITM proxy that > silently intercepts and decrypts the "secure" E2E conversation by swapping RSA public keys.
concepts: Built `mitm.py` — full TCP MITM proxy with RSA key swap and AES-GCM session interception
Wrote `mitm_addon.py` — mitmproxy professional tool integration
Tested the complete Act 2 flow: Alice → MITM → Bob, with MITM reading all messages
Captured the "money shot" screenshot: Alice's plaintext message visible in MITM terminal while Bob receives what appears to be a normal encrypted chat
Debugged and resolved the thread synchronization problem using `threading.Event`
topics: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 2 Complete: MITM Key Swap
How the MITM Key Swap Attack Works
Challenge Solved — Cross-Thread State Sharing (Session Pairing)

entries.title: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 3: Fingerprint Verification Defeats MITM
entries.summary: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 3: Fingerprint Verification Defeats MITM > Project Context: Day 4. Today's goal was to implement the *defense* — showing how out-of-band > key fingerprint verification completely defeats the MITM key swap attack. This mirrors exactly
concepts: Added `verified` mode to `alice.py` and `bob.py` with dual-fingerprint display
Demonstrated Act 3 with MITM running: fingerprints mismatch → session correctly aborts
Demonstrated Act 3 without MITM (`--direct` flag, port 9000): fingerprints match → secure chat proceeds
Understood and applied the exact mechanism that production E2E apps use to solve the key verification problem
topics: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Act 3: Fingerprint Verification Defeats MITM

concepts: Extended `mitm.py` with a Flask REST API on port 7000
Built `dashboard/index.html` — dark-themed, auto-polling, color-coded attacker dashboard
Ran full Act 2 demo with dashboard open — confirmed real-time event feed updates correctly
Captured dashboard screenshot showing live decrypted message interception
topics: E2E Encryption Attack Lab — Day 5: Live Attacker Dashboard
Flask as a Background API Server
Threading Model — Flask + TCP Proxy Coexistence
Challenge Solved — Flask Blocking the TCP Proxy

entries.title: VaultZero — Zero-Knowledge Password Manager (Project Planning)

Mapped out the full feature set across all target platforms (extension, Android, iOS)

Outlined the honest limitations of the approach (client-side malware)

entries.title: VaultZero — Cryptographic Architecture Research & Tech Stack Decisions
entries.summary: Day 0 of VaultZero — before writing a single line of code.
concepts: Designed the complete zero-knowledge threat model for VaultZero
Evaluated and chose Argon2id over bcrypt/scrypt with documented rationale
Designed the two-layer key architecture (KEK + Vault Key) to allow password changes without vault re-encryption
Chose npm workspaces over Turborepo/Nx for monorepo management — documented the trade-offs
Designed the 5-model Prisma schema (User, VaultBlob, Device, AuditLog, RefreshToken)
Mapped out the complete 93-file folder structure for the monorepo
topics: VaultZero — Cryptographic Architecture Research & Tech Stack Decisions
Why Zero-Knowledge? The Threat Model First
Key Derivation — Why Argon2id over bcrypt/scrypt

entries.title: VaultZero — Day 1: Monorepo Scaffold, Prisma Schema & Repository Setup
entries.summary: Day 1 of the 14-day VaultZero build.
concepts: Initialised the full npm workspaces monorepo with 4 packages (`core`, `backend`, `extension`, `web`)
Created all 93 files — package configs, TypeScript configs, Jest configs, esbuild config, Manifest V3, folder structure stubs
Wrote the complete Prisma schema: `User`, `VaultBlob`, `Device`, `AuditLog`, `RefreshToken` with all relations and field mappings
Configured root-level scripts for building, running, migrating, and testing all workspaces with a single command
Verified the entire scaffold — 0 TypeScript errors, Prisma client generated, all 93 files pushed to GitHub
topics: VaultZero — Day 1: Monorepo Scaffold, Prisma Schema & Repository Setup
npm Workspaces — How the Monorepo Is Wired
The Prisma Schema — 5 Models, Every Field Intentional
Folder Structure — What Each Package Does
Security Rules Baked in from Day 1

entries.title: VaultZero — Day 2: Crypto Core — Argon2id, AES-256-GCM & Key Management
entries.summary: Day 2 of the 14-day VaultZero build.
concepts: Implemented `argon2.ts` — Argon2id key derivation using libsodium, 64 MB memory-hard, CSPRNG salt generation
Implemented `aes-gcm.ts` — AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption using WebCrypto, fresh IV per call, deliberate error message collapsing
Implemented `keys.ts` — vault key generation, wrap (encrypt with KEK), and unwrap (decrypt with KEK)
Exported all crypto primitives through `core/src/crypto/index.ts` and the package barrel `core/src/index.ts`
Verified `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors across all crypto files
topics: VaultZero — Day 2: Crypto Core — Argon2id, AES-256-GCM & Key Management
Why WebCrypto () not libsodium for AES-GCM?

entries.title: VaultZero — Day 3: Vault Operations, Recovery Phrase & Encoding Utilities
entries.summary: Day 3 of the 14-day VaultZero build.
concepts: Defined `VaultEntry`, `Vault`, and `EncryptedVault` TypeScript interfaces with `readonly` fields and schema versioning
Implemented `encryptVault` and `decryptVault` — full vault serialise → UTF-8 → AES-256-GCM → base64 pipeline
Implemented BIP-39 recovery phrase generation (256-bit entropy, SHA-256 checksum, 2048-word wordlist)
Implemented all encoding helpers — `toBase64`, `fromBase64`, `toHex`, `fromHex`, `toUTF8Bytes`, `fromUTF8Bytes`
Implemented `constantTimeEqual` for timing-safe hash comparisons
Wired all modules through barrel `index.ts` files — `@vaultzero/core` is now a complete, importable library
Verified `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors across all core files
topics: VaultZero — Day 3: Vault Operations, Recovery Phrase & Encoding Utilities
— Encrypt and Decrypt the Full Vault

entries.title: Cybersecurity Portfolio — Day 1: Tech Stack, Design System & Core Structure
entries.summary: Day 1 of building a production-grade cybersecurity portfolio from scratch.
concepts: Bootstrapped the TanStack Start + Vite 7 + React 19 + TypeScript 5.8 project
Configured Tailwind CSS v4 with `@theme` directive and OKLCH design tokens
Defined the full color palette: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary` (neon cyan), `--accent` (neon magenta)
Set up Google Fonts: Space Grotesk (headings) and JetBrains Mono (body/code)
Implemented 4 semantic utility classes: `.text-gradient`, `.glass`, `.neon-border`, `.grid-bg`
Built `__root.tsx` with SEO meta tags (title, description, OG tags)
Built the Navbar with scroll-aware glass effect using passive scroll listener
Built the CustomCursor using `requestAnimationFrame` loop + `mix-blend-mode: difference`
Installed full shadcn/ui component library (Radix UI primitives)
Set up react-hook-form + zod, react-query, GSAP 3 + ScrollTrigger

entries.title: Cybersecurity Portfolio — Day 2: Hero Section, 3D Globe (HeroScene), About, Projects & Skills
entries.summary: Day 2 of the portfolio build.
concepts: Built the Hero section: gradient name headline, monospace bio, 2 CTA buttons, 4-stat grid, animated scroll indicator
Built `HeroScene.tsx` — complete WebGL globe with IcosahedronGeometry wireframe at detail 4
Placed 25 real-world city nodes using spherical lat/lon → Cartesian coordinate transform
Generated Bézier arcs between the nearest 3 cities per node using `QuadraticBezierCurve3`
Animated 40 data packets traversing arcs per-frame in `useFrame()`, using a reusable `THREE.Object3D` dummy to avoid per-frame allocations
Implemented cursor speed tracking → arc opacity and globe rotation modulation
Built the About section with GSAP ScrollTrigger stagger reveal (`gsap.context()` for safe cleanup)
Built 3 glassmorphism info cards (Focus / Currently / Education)
Built the Projects section with 4 real project cards, scroll-in animations, hover lift, external link icon
Built the Skills section with 26 tools across 4 categories as neon-bordered monospace chips
topics: Cybersecurity Portfolio — Day 2: Hero Section, 3D Globe (HeroScene), About, Projects & Skills
HeroScene — Custom WebGL Globe with Three.js

entries.title: Cybersecurity Portfolio — Day 3: Light Mode — Design Research & CSS Architecture
entries.summary: Day 3 of the portfolio build.
concepts: Designed the complete light-mode OKLCH color palette with perceptually uniform lightness values
Mapped every design token (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--accent`, `--muted`, `--border`) to light-mode equivalents
Understood why neon accents must shift from high-lightness to mid-lightness for light backgrounds (WCAG contrast requirements)
Planned the `[data-theme="light"]` CSS cascade architecture — no JavaScript color swaps, pure CSS variable overrides
Designed the light-mode glassmorphism variant — increased blur radius to compensate for reduced contrast
Planned the `ThemeToggle` component with `localStorage` persistence and FOUT prevention via inline script
Researched WCAG AA contrast ratios for the new `--primary` teal against the `--background` off-white
topics: Cybersecurity Portfolio — Day 3: Light Mode — Design Research & CSS Architecture
Why Light Mode Is Non-Trivial for a Dark-First Site
CSS Custom Properties — Theme Switching Architecture

entries.title: Cybersecurity Portfolio — Day 4: Light Mode — Full Implementation & Deployment
entries.summary: Day 4 of the portfolio build.
concepts: Implemented the full `[data-theme="light"]` CSS token override block — 6 design tokens
Wrote light-mode overrides for `.glass`, `.neon-border`, and `.grid-bg` utility classes
Built `ThemeToggle.tsx` component with `localStorage` persistence, `aria-label`, and smooth hover animation
Implemented FOUT prevention via inline `<script>` in `<head>` before stylesheets
Built `useTheme` hook using `MutationObserver` to reactively feed theme state to WebGL components
Updated `HeroScene.tsx` to use the `useTheme` hook for dynamic Three.js material colors
Integrated `ThemeToggle` into the Navbar alongside the nav links
Deployed to Vercel and manually verified all 7 acceptance criteria on the live site
